columns.name: normalized_question
columns.system_prompt: You rewrite real GitHub issue / PR threads into concise support questions. Preserve technical fidelity: do not invent facts.

columns.name: support_answer
columns.system_prompt: You write grounded Unsloth support answers. Faithful to the thread, no invented facts, no em-dashes, no emojis, no AI-disclaimer phrases.

nodes.name: note_1
nodes.markdown:
### GitHub Support Bot
Real GitHub data to synthetic Q&A pairs for support-bot fine-tuning.

**Click `Run` below to generate 10 sample rows.** Defaults already point at `unslothai/unsloth` + `unslothai/unsloth-zoo`, use the server's `GH_TOKEN` env var, and run the bundled local model.

**Upgrade to production**
- Swap `unsloth/gemma-4-E2B-it-GGUF` for a larger model in the model_config node.
- Replace the demo prompts on `normalized_question` / `support_answer` with the canonical codex pattern below (see Note 3).
- Raise the seed `limit` from 100 to `0` (All) for a full backfill.
- Raise `max_parallel_requests` back to 4 once your inference server can handle it.

nodes.name: note_2
nodes.markdown:
The **normalize** step turns a raw GitHub thread (which often has title/body/comments scattered) into a single clean support question.

Tweak the prompt to:
- always include the traceback
- drop off-topic chitchat
- target a specific user persona

nodes.name: note_3
nodes.markdown:
The **answer** block produces `{answer, diagnosis_questions, cites, confidence}`, ready to feed into the grounded RAG answerer.

**Demo default**: 80-200 word answer, one inline `[source: <url>]` cite, `max_parallel_requests=1` so a small local model stays stable.

**Production prompt (paste in):**
- Require 150-300 word answers with 2+ citations.
- Enforce named symbols (function / flag / env var / file path).
- Code fences for every procedural step.
- Use the resolved-issue / merged-PR template: `What changed / Fix location / Minimum repro`.
- Reject rows containing em-dashes, emojis, or AI-disclaimer phrases.

See the Unsloth SupportBot dataset card for the full codex prompt we used to train `Gemma-4-Unsloth-Bot`.
